SEARCH
モジュール org.commonutils

パッケージ org.commonutils.annotation

package org.commonutils.annotation
Marker annotations documenting nullability and numeric ranges on this library's public API. They serve as human- and tool-readable contracts; validating methods in org.commonutils.lang and org.commonutils.util enforce the contracts at runtime where stated (see each annotation).

Library consumers: these annotations do not implement validation. Applying them to your own fields, parameters, methods, or types does not cause commonutils to enforce anything at runtime; add your own checks or rely on static analysis or other tooling as needed. Documented NullPointerException and IllegalArgumentException behavior applies only where this library's public methods explicitly validate arguments (see each method in org.commonutils.lang and org.commonutils.util).

Annotations

  • Nullable / NonNull — reference may or must not be null
  • NonNegative / Positive — numeric range expectations
  • 注釈インタフェース
    クラス
    説明
    NonNegative
    Marks a numeric type usage intended to be zero or greater: primitive integrals (int, long, …) — >= 0; non-NaN floating-point — >= 0.0.
    NonNull
    Marks a type usage whose value must not be null.
    Nullable
    Marks a type usage whose value may be null.
    Positive
    Marks a numeric type usage intended to be strictly positive.
